checks.a_analysis: The conversion results in the 'semantic.json' output do not contain any fabricated paragraphs or sentences. The section titles (e.g., 'Standards Committee Agenda October 2021 PDF', 'Standards Meeting Minutes January') are AI-generated for accessibility, which is an acceptable change. The body text in each section accurately reflects the content found in the source PDF.

All contact information, including the mailing addresses for Jason P. Steward and the Standards Committee on page 5, has been correctly transcribed and matches the source document exactly. No fabricated phone numbers, emails, or URLs were detected.

All seven pages of the source PDF are represented in the output. Page 1 (Agenda), Pages 2-4 (Minutes and Votes), Page 5 (Resignation), Page 6 (Goals), and Page 7 (Dates) are all present within the structured sections of the JSON.

Two tables in the output are missing a material number of rows. The 'Meeting Attendees January 7, 2021' table in Section 2 (source page 2) contains only 1 data row in the JSON despite the source image showing approximately 23 rows of committee members and staff. Additionally, the 'Roll Call Votes' table in Section 2 (source page 4) is entirely empty of data rows in the JSON, whereas the source document records votes for 11 different members. Both instances exceed the 20% threshold for missing rows.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 2: 'Attendance' table contains ~23 rows (Marty Cooke through Angie Turbeville). Page 4: 'ROLL CALL VOTES' table contains rows for 11 members (Marty Cooke through Donna Wright).
checks.b_evidence_output: Section 'Standards Meeting Minutes January', element index 4 (Attendance table) contains only one data row ('Commissioner Marty Cooke'). Element index 9 (Roll Call Votes table) contains no data rows.
checks.b_evidence_location: Section 'Standards Meeting Minutes January', Page 2 and Page 4

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: Section 2 ('Standards Meeting Minutes January'), which covers pages 2, 3, and 4 of the source, suffers from a significant omission of content due to the failure to capture the data rows in the attendance and voting tables. This missing data constitutes a large portion (estimated >30%) of the total informational content intended for that specific section, resulting in a substantial loss of fidelity compared to the source.
checks.b_evidence_source: Section 2 (Pages 2-4) includes attendance and vote records for 23 and 11 individuals respectively, which are integral to the meeting minutes documentation.
checks.b_evidence_output: The structured JSON for Section 2 omits 22 out of 23 attendance records and all 11 roll call vote records, retaining only high-level headers and point-form minutes.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text content that was successfully captured (Agenda items, Minutes list, Resignation letter, Committee Goals, and Meeting Dates) accurately reflects the wording of the source PDF. Minor OCR discrepancies (e.g., 'Tine' instead of 'Tina') are acceptable given the scanned nature of parts of the source document.

checks.a_analysis: The content in the output appears only once, corresponding to its single appearance in the source PDF. There are no duplicated paragraphs, tables, or lists beyond acceptable metadata and image alt-text repetitions.

The output document consistently uses Heading 3 (H3) for all major section titles (e.g., 'heading-1', 'heading-2', 'heading-4'). There are no Heading 1 (H1) or Heading 2 (H2) elements present within the sections. This represents a break in the heading hierarchy as it skips the primary and secondary levels required for logical navigation.
checks.b_evidence_source: The document contains main titles and section headers like 'North Carolina 911 Board Standards Committee Meeting Agenda' and 'Standards Committee Goals'.

checks.a_analysis: Two tables in the output are severely truncated. The 'Attendance' table on page 2 of the source contains over 20 entries for committee members and staff, but the output table only contains a single data row (Commissioner Marty Cooke). Similarly, the 'ROLL CALL VOTES' table on page 4 of the source contains 11 rows of voting data, while the output table contains only the header row and zero data rows. This loss of structural data constitutes a major corruption.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 2 and Page 4 of the source PDF contain detailed tables for Attendance and Roll Call Votes with multiple rows of data.
checks.b_evidence_output: The table at sections[1].elements[4] contains only one data row for Marty Cooke. The table at sections[1].elements[7] contains only a header row.

checks.a_analysis: The reading order in the output generally follows the top-to-bottom flow of the source PDF. Content from different pages (Agenda, Minutes, Letter, Goals, Dates) is sequenced logically.
The lists found on page 3 (Minutes) and page 6 (Goals) have been correctly identified and converted into ordered lists, preserving their sequence and meaning.
There are no links present in the source document, and none have been fabricated or injected into the output.

checks.a_analysis: The overall visual layout of the document is preserved. The single-column flow of the HTML correctly follows the sequential organization of the source PDF's pages (Agenda, Attendees, Minutes, Votes, Correspondence, Goals, and Dates). While fonts and margins differ, the logical arrangement of sections is consistent with the source.

There are two major table rendering failures. First, the 'ROLL CALL VOTES' table from page 4 of the source PDF is completely broken; only the header text is rendered, while the entire data grid (11 member rows and their 'Y' votes) is missing. Second, the HTML version of the attendance table under 'Meeting Attendees January 7, 2021' is truncated, showing only one row ('Commissioner Marty Cooke') whereas the source contains 23 staff and committee member entries.
checks.b_evidence_source: The ROLL CALL VOTES table on page 4 contains a grid of 11 members with 'Y' indicators for two separate votes. The Attendance table on page 2 contains 23 rows of members and staff.
checks.b_evidence_output: The section 'Roll Call Votes' contains only the header text as a paragraph. The table under 'Meeting Attendees January 7, 2021' contains only one row for Commissioner Marty Cooke.
checks.b_evidence_location: Roll Call Votes section (corresponding to PDF page 4) and Meeting Attendees section (corresponding to PDF page 2).

checks.a_analysis: Several meaningful logos present in the source document are missing in the HTML conversion. Specifically, the 'NC DIT' logo from page 4 (Roll Call Votes) and the large 'NCDIT | North Carolina Department of Information Technology' logo from the top of page 7 (2022 Standards Committee Dates) are absent from their respective sections in the rendered output.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 7 features a large branding logo for the North Carolina Department of Information Technology at the top of the page. Page 4 also contains the NC DIT 911 Board logo.
checks.b_evidence_output: The section '2022 Standards Committee Dates' begins directly with text, omitting the logo. The 'Roll Call Votes' section also lacks the logo present in the source.
checks.b_evidence_location: Section '2022 Standards Committee Dates' (PDF page 7) and 'Roll Call Votes' (PDF page 4).

checks.a_analysis: The content from PDF page 2 (Draft Minutes header and Attendance table) is visually duplicated in the HTML. It appears first as a large formatted block/image under the heading 'Standards Meeting Minutes January' and is immediately repeated as text and a partial HTML table under the 'Meeting Attendees' section.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 2 contains the header 'North Carolina 911 Board Standards Committee Meeting Draft Minutes...' and the Attendance table.
checks.b_evidence_output: This same set of information (Minutes header and Attendance list) appears twice: once as an image-like block and again as text/table content in the following section.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections 'Standards Meeting Minutes January' and 'North Carolina 911 Board Standards Committee Meeting Draft Minutes...'
